summary: The web-app-2 OpenSpec artifacts are internally consistent, directly address the explicit goal, and are safe to proceed to implementation. All four documents (proposal, design, spec, tasks) are aligned. No blocking issues found.

recommendations.description: The design specifies 'bounded automatic polling' as fallback but does not quantify the polling interval or maximum retry window. Without bounds defined, implementations may choose intervals that either hammer the backend or leave operators with unacceptably stale data. Recommend adding a concrete upper-bound (e.g., max interval 30s, backoff cap) so implementations converge on safe behavior.

No scenario covers initial-load when the push transport is unavailable from the start (stream never connects, not just disconnects after initial snapshot). The existing 'Stream falls back safely' scenario presupposes an established session. An additional scenario for startup-time fallback would close this gap and ensure implementations handle the cold-start case explicitly.

Tasks are unordered and carry no dependency annotations. The schema definition task (task 1) is a prerequisite for transport, state merging, and UI tasks. Marking dependencies or numbering by suggested sequence would reduce integration risk during implementation.

test_gaps.description: No test scenario covers simultaneous pushed updates to multiple rounds while the operator has a single round detail view open. The 'Selected round context is preserved' scenario only checks that selection is retained, not that the incoming round-list updates are merged correctly without corrupting the detail view state.
test_gaps.suggested_scenario: GIVEN the operator is viewing round detail for round A; WHEN pushed updates arrive for rounds B, C, and also for round A; THEN round A detail updates in place, the round list reflects B and C changes, and no round-list update causes round A to deselect or its detail to reload.

test_gaps.description: No test scenario covers the initial page load when push transport is unavailable from startup (cold-start fallback). Current 'Stream falls back safely' scenario requires an established session before fallback triggers.
test_gaps.suggested_scenario: GIVEN the push endpoint is unreachable before the operator opens the app; WHEN the app loads; THEN it falls back to polling immediately, labels the state as fallback mode, and delivers initial snapshot data without blocking the operator.

test_gaps.description: The duplicate/replay scenario for inbox is implicit via the general feed dedup requirement but is not an explicit scenario in the Inbox And Notification Updates requirement. Inbox grouping logic could create duplicate group headers on replay; this edge case warrants a dedicated scenario.

proposal.md.notes: Clear motivation, appropriate in/out-of-scope boundaries, success criteria map directly to the explicit goal. Read-only and no-spend constraints are explicit.

design.md.notes: Update model is comprehensive with typed events and stable IDs. Feed ordering and scroll-context preservation are well-specified. Operator UI principles are restrained and work-focused. Transport flexibility is preserved. Source-of-truth authority is unambiguous. Only gap is unquantified polling bounds (see rec-01).

specs/web-app-hub/spec.md.notes: Modified and added requirements are well-structured with given/when/then scenarios. Read Only Push Updates requirement explicitly prohibits mutations. Implementation Stack Flexibility is properly formalized. Three test gaps noted but none are blocking.

tasks.md.notes: Eight tasks cover schema, transport, state merging, UI, error states, and verification. Coverage is complete. Ordering/dependency annotation would improve implementation guidance (see rec-03).

explicit_goal_coverage.push_content_dynamic_updates: fully addressed — Automatic Data Delivery requirement with three scenarios
explicit_goal_coverage.newest_first_feeds_no_refresh: fully addressed — Newest First Operational Feeds requirement with three scenarios including scroll-away and replay
explicit_goal_coverage.alternative_languages_and_frameworks: fully addressed — Implementation Stack Flexibility requirement with two scenarios
explicit_goal_coverage.professional_operator_focused_ui: fully addressed — Professional Operator Interface requirement with three scenarios covering overview, scanning, and narrow viewports
